name: Analyzing Code Complexity — Step by Step

metadata.career_relevance: The Razorpay interviewer hands you code and asks 'What's the complexity?' in 30 seconds. This topic gives you the systematic process to answer any code correctly — loops, recursion, nested calls, everything.

hook.what_you_will_build: A systematic framework to analyze ANY code — loops, recursion, sorting calls, method chains.

visual_aid.description: Recursion tree for merge sort. Root: T(n) splits into two T(n/2), each splits into two T(n/4), down to T(1) leaves. Each level: work = n (merge step). Number of levels = log n. Total work = n × log n = O(n log n). Labeled: 'Level 0: n work, Level 1: n/2 + n/2 = n work, Level log n: 1+1+...=n work. Total = n × log n levels'.
visual_aid.alt_text: Merge sort recursion tree showing O(n log n) derivation

worked_example.scenario: Analyze processPayments() from the hook — step by step.
worked_example.problem_statement: Determine Big-O of: sort(n) + loop building HashMap + nested reconcile calls.

step_by_step_solution.details: Collections.sort() uses TimSort. Dominant cost for many interview answers.

things_to_notice: Collections.sort() hidden cost: it's O(n log n) — often the dominant term in data-processing code.
Sum over all merchants: if each merchant m has mᵢ payments and Σmᵢ = n, then Σ(mᵢ log mᵢ) ≤ n log n.
Method calls cost: analyze WHAT the method does — a method call isn't always O(1).

industry_examples.use_case: Every code review includes complexity analysis. Senior engineers reject PRs where O(n²) queries run per HTTP request.

applications.interview_relevance: The systematic framework: identify expensive operations → express in terms of n → drop constants → state with justification. Takes 30-60 seconds with practice.
applications.real_world_workflow: Code review: annotate each block with its Big-O as comments. Flag any O(n²) in a hot path.

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Treating all method calls as O(1). `list.contains(x)` inside a loop is O(n) per call → O(n²) total.
common_gaps.remediation: For every method call inside a loop: look up its complexity. contains() on ArrayList = O(n). Same on HashSet = O(1). The container choice changes the outer loop's complexity.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Student says 'just one loop, so O(n)' but loop body calls ArrayList.contains()

key_takeaways: 5-step framework: identify → count → combine → drop → state
Method calls inside loops: O(m) per call × O(n) outer = O(nm)
Recurrence T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n) → O(n log n) — merge sort signature
Recurrence T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1) → O(log n) — binary search signature
Sort is often the dominant term: O(n log n) beats O(n) over n iterations of O(log n)

self_check_questions.question: What is the complexity of: `for x in list: if x in hashSet: count++`?
self_check_questions.answer: O(n). The loop runs n times. hashSet.contains() is O(1) per call. n × O(1) = O(n). If it were ArrayList.contains() instead of HashSet: O(n) per call → O(n²) total. Data structure choice changes complexity.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Space Complexity — how much memory does your algorithm use? The other half of every interview answer.
